title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Term Insurance With Money Back – Yes, It Is Possible
Added Word Count: 840
URL: https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/term/term-insurance-with-money-back.html
Generally, people are familiar with term insurance as a means to provide pure life cover, safeguarding their family in their absence. But do you know that you can also get something if you outlive the policy tenure? This is only possible if you’ve chosen a term plan with a return of premium option. It combines financial protection under a term plan with the return of the premiums paid on maturity. In this article, we’ll explore in detail term insurance with money back plans, how they work, what their benefits are, and how they differ from a regular term plan.
What is term insurance?
Term insurance is the purest form of life insurance. It is essentially a type of a life insurance policy that provides the nominees or beneficiaries a death benefit in the event of the policyholder’s demise. In exchange for the life insurance cover, the policyholder is required to make regular payments known as "premiums" to the insurance company.
Unlike other types of life insurance plans, the pay-out in a term insurance is only made in the case of the policyholder’s death during the policy term and not upon the term policy’s maturity if the holder survives the tenure. It is primarily for this reason that the premiums charged for term insurance is always lower than the other types of life insurance.
However, now you can also buy term insurance plans that provide a return of premium on maturity, which means you will receive the money you have paid in your term insurance policy if you live the term of the policy.
What is the money-back term plan policy?
Now that you have the answer to the question "What is term insurance?" let us take a quick look at what the return of premium/money back feature is.
As you have already seen above, a term insurance is a life insurance policy. Since the policyholder is not liable to receive any maturity benefits if he survives until the end of the policy term, the premiums that he pays during the tenure of the policy is essentially forfeited and is not returned to him (excluding taxes on premiums). However if there is a sudden demise of the policyholder during the policy term, the nominee stands to receive the term insurance benefits.
On the other hand, there is a variation of a term policy where the insurance company returns the premiums paid throughout the tenure of the policy back to the policyholder. This is known as a money back term plan. These premiums (excluding taxes), which is paid throughout the tenure, is returned if the policyholder survives until the end of the policy term.
In the event of his death during the policy tenure, the beneficiaries of the policyholder would be eligible for a death benefit in accordance with the conditions of the term policy. Such a policy is also known as term insurance with money back or a term return of premium (TROP) plan.
How does term insurance with money back Work?
Here is a brief look at how term insurance with return of premium works -
Let us assume that Mr. Rakesh is in the market for a term policy. He purchases a term insurance with the money back option, which provides a life insurance cover of around Rs. 50 lakhs. The total tenure of the term policy is 20 years. In exchange for the life cover, he is required to pay an amount of Rs. 10,000 per annum (exclusive of the taxes) for the entire term of 20 years. In this case, one of two things are likely to happen. Let us visit each scenario one after the other -
Scenario 1: Mr. Rakesh dies during the policy term
In this scenario, the nominees or the beneficiaries of Mr. Rakesh would receive the sum of Rs. 50 lakhs as a death benefit from the insurance company.
Scenario 2: Mr. Rakesh survives until the end of the policy term
In such a situation, Mr. Rakesh would get back the premium that he paid throughout the entire tenure of the policy, which comes up to Rs. 2,00,000 (Rs. 10,000 x 20 years).
Benefits of money-back term insurance
Term insurance with money back option comes with a whole host of benefits to the policyholder. Here is a quick glimpse of some of the advantages –
Guaranteed* return of premiums
With regular term insurance plans, the policyholder does not receive any maturity benefits. However, with TROP plans, they get to enjoy a maturity benefit by way of getting their premiums back.
Tax benefits
The premium that the policyholder gets back at the end of a TROP plan is completely exempted from taxation under section 10(1 0D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, subject to the provisions stated therein. This is in addition to the tax deduction of Rs. 1,50,000 that he gets to enjoy on the premiums paid towards the term policy according to section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961.
Surrender value
In the event where the policyholder surrenders a TROP plan, he is liable to get back a portion of the premiums paid by him if the policy has acquired any surrender value at the time of surrender, subject to policy terms and conditions.
Advantages of rider options
Optional riders like critical illness, accidental death benefit, waiver of premium, etc., are available with such plans. You can add these riders at a nominal additional premium to enhance the coverage of your policy.
Can Anyone Apply for a Term Plan with Return of Premium?
It is recommended to buy a term insurance to financially safeguard your family in the case of your premature demise. With a term plan with return of premium, you get something extra in the form of a maturity benefit. You can buy a term plan with return of premium depending on your needs. Here’s how this plan can be helpful at different stages of life:
Unmarried Individual
If you’re single, you may still support dependent parents or younger siblings. A term plan with return of premium can help you secure your dependents’ finances. If something unfortunate happens to you during the policy tenure, your family gets a death benefit, which they can use to meet their lifestyle expenses or clear debts. Alternatively, if you survive the policy term, the premiums are refunded, which can give you a corpus for your financial needs or goals.
Married but no children
If you’re married and your spouse is financially dependent on you, a TROP cover will be helpful. It can provide your spouse with the death benefit in case of your sudden demise during the policy tenure. If the ‘risk’ doesn’t arise, you get a refund of all the premiums paid.
Married person with children
A term plan with return of premium is more meaningful for someone with kids. The death benefit ensures that your children’s education and other daily needs of life are not affected when you’re no longer around. The return of premium feature on outliving the policy term can help you fulfil your child’s future needs.
Difference Between Pure Term Insurance and Term Plans with Return of Premium
Some common differences between a pure term insurance and a term plan with return of premium are covered below:
Key Takeaway
A pure term insurance provides basic life cover.
Term with return of premium provides insurance coverage and a refund of premiums on outliving the policy.
The return of premium term plan also provides tax benefits under Section 80C and Section 10(10D).
TROP is suitable for people of all ages with coverage options chosen as per income, lifestyle habits, and financial goals.
Premiums are higher in a TROP policy as it provides a premium refund feature.
Conclusion
A term insurance with a money back option is a good option for individuals, since it provides a lot of value to the policyholder. With TROP plans, the policyholder gets to enjoy a guaranteed* return of the premiums that he paid. This reduces the financial burden significantly, which in turn gives the holder some much-needed peace of mind.
If you opt for a term plan with a return of premium option, make sure you pay your premiums regularly. That way, you will continue to enjoy the life cover, and in case you survive the policy term, all the premiums you paid will be returned, so you can count on that lump sum inflow of funds at the end of the tenure.
FAQs
Are There Any Riders Available With TROP?
Yes, riders like critical illness, waiver of premium, or accidental death benefit can be availed with a TROP plan at a nominal additional premium. However, the maturity amount paid on surviving the policy will not include the rider costs.
Is term insurance with a return of premium worth buying?
A TROP plan combines protection and savings under the same plan. It’s a favourable choice if your budget allows, and the psychological comfort of getting premium back on survival matters.
Can unmarried individuals buy term insurance with return of premium?
Unmarried individuals can buy term insurance with return of premium if they’ve dependent parents or siblings. The sum assured on death during policy tenure will provide the financial support. The refund of premiums on living till the end of the policy can give them a financial corpus for their needs.
Does term insurance with return of premium offer tax benefits?
Yes. Premiums paid under TROP qualify for deduction under Section 80C and the payout on maturity is exempt under Section 10(10D), provided certain conditions are met.
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Term Insurance Plan along With Money Back – Yes, It Is Possible
Added Word Count: 840
URL: https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/term/term-insurance-with-money- back.html
Generally, people are familiar with term insurance as a means to provide pure life cover, safeguarding their family in their absence. But do you know that you can also get a maturity benefit if you outlive the policy tenure? This is only possible if you’ve chosen a term plan with a return of premium option. It combines financial protection under a term plan with the return of the premiums paid on maturity. In this article, we’ll explore in detail term insurance with money back plans, how they work, what their benefits are, and how they differ from a regular term plan.
What is term insurance?
Term insurance is the purest form of life insurance. It is essentially a type of a life insurance policy that provides the nominees or beneficiaries a death benefit in the event of the life assured’s demise. In exchange for the life insurance cover, the policyholder is required to make regular payments known as "premiums" to the insurance company.
The pay-out in a term insurance is only made in the case of the policyholder’s death during the policy term and not upon the term policy’s maturity if the holder survives the tenure. It is primarily for this reason that the premiums charged for term insurance is always lower than the other types of life insurance.
However, now you can also buy term insurance plans that provide a return of premium on maturity, which means you will receive the money you have paid in your term insurance policy if you live the term of the policy.
What is the term plan with ROP policy?
Now that you have the answer to the question "What is term insurance?" let us take a quick look at what the return of premium/money back feature is.
As you have already seen above, a term insurance is a life insurance policy. Since the policyholder is not liable to receive any maturity benefits if the life assured survives until the end of the policy term, the premiums that the policyholder pays during the tenure of the policy is essentially forfeited and is not returned to him (excluding taxes on premiums). However if there is a sudden demise of the life assured during the policy term, the nominee stands to receive the term insurance benefits.
On the other hand, there is a variation of a term policy where the insurance company returns the premiums paid throughout the tenure of the policy back to the policyholder. This is known as a term plan with ROP. These premiums (excluding taxes), which is paid throughout the tenure, is returned if the life assured survives until the end of the policy term.
In the event of the death of the life assured during the policy tenure, the nominees would be eligible for a death benefit in accordance with the conditions of the term policy. Such a policy is also known as term insurance with return of premium (TROP) plan.
How does term insurance with ROP Work?
Here is a brief look at how term insurance with return of premium works -
Let us assume that Mr. Rakesh is in the market for a term policy. He purchases a term insurance with the money back option, which provides a life insurance cover of around Rs. 50 lakhs. The total tenure of the term policy is 20 years. In exchange for the life cover, he is required to pay an amount of Rs. 10,000 per annum (exclusive of the taxes) for the entire term of 20 years. In this case, one of two things are likely to happen. Let us visit each scenario one after the other -
● Scenario 1: Mr. Rakesh dies during the policy term
In this scenario, the nominees or the beneficiaries of Mr. Rakesh would receive the sum of Rs. 50 lakhs as a death benefit from the insurance company.
● Scenario 2: Mr. Rakesh survives until the end of the policy term
In such a situation, Mr. Rakesh would get back the premium that he paid throughout the entire tenure of the policy, which comes up to Rs. 2,00,000 (Rs. 10,000 x 20 years).
Benefits of term insurance with ROP
Term insurance with money back option comes with a whole host of benefits to the policyholder. Here is a quick glimpse of some of the advantages –
1. Return of premiums
With regular term insurance plans, the policyholder does not receive any maturity benefits. However, with TROP plans, they get to enjoy a maturity benefit by way of getting their premiums back.
2. Tax benefits
The premium that the policyholder gets back at the end of a TROP plan is completely exempted from taxation under section 10(1 0D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, subject to the provisions stated therein. This is in addition to the tax deduction of Rs. 1,50,000 that he gets to enjoy on the premiums paid towards the term policy according to section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961.
3. Surrender value
In the event where the policyholder surrenders a TROP plan, he is liable to get back a portion of the premiums paid by him if the policy has acquired any surrender value at the time of surrender, subject to policy terms and conditions.
4. Advantages of rider options
Optional riders like critical illness rider, accidental death benefit rider, waiver of premium rider, etc., are available with such plans. You can add these riders at a nominal additional premium to enhance the coverage of your policy.
Who Can Apply for a Term Plan with Return of Premium?
It is recommended to buy a term insurance to financially safeguard your family in the case of your premature demise. With a term plan with return of premium, you get something extra in the form of a maturity benefit. You can buy a term plan with return of premium depending on your financial needs. Here’s how this plan can be helpful at different stages of life:
1. Unmarried Individual
If you’re single, you may still support dependent parents or younger siblings. A term plan with return of premium can help you secure your dependents’ finances. If something unfortunate happens to you during the policy tenure, your family gets a death benefit, which they can use to meet their lifestyle expenses or clear debts. Alternatively, if you survive the policy term, the premiums are refunded, which can give you a corpus for your financial needs or goals.
2. Married but no children
If you’re married and your spouse is financially dependent on you, a TROP cover will be helpful. It can provide your spouse with the death benefit in case of your sudden demise during the policy tenure. If the life assured does not pass away during the policy tenure, you get a refund of all the premiums paid.
3. Married person with children
A term plan with return of premium is more meaningful for someone with kids. The death benefit can help with your children’s education and other daily needs of life are not affected when you’re no longer around. The return of premium feature on outliving the policy term can help you fulfil your child’s future needs.
Difference Between Pure Term Insurance and Term Plans with Return of Premium
Some common differences between a pure term insurance and a term plan with return of premium are covered below:
Aspect Pure Term Plan Term Plan with Return of Premium Basic Plan It provides life coverage. It provides life coverage and returns the premiums paid if you survive the policy tenure. Benefits Covered Death benefit, i.e. the sum assured, is provided to the nominee if you pass away during the policy term.
It covers death benefit and refund of premiums on survival.
Premium Cost The premiums are lower and affordable.
The premiums are higher than a term plan, as it also provides a maturity benefit
Suitable For If you’re looking only for financial security for your family at an affordable cost, it’s a favourable choice.
If you want both financial protection and return of premiums , TROP can be a good choice
Key Takeaway
● A pure term insurance provides basic life cover. ● Term insurance with return of premium provides life insurance coverage and a refund of premiums on outliving the policy. ● The return of premium term plan also provides tax benefits under Section 80C under the old tax regime and Section 10(10D). ● TROP is suitable for people of all ages with coverage options chosen as per income, lifestyle habits, and financial goals. ● Premiums are higher in a TROP policy as it provides a return of premiums feature.
Conclusion
A term insurance with a money back option is a good option for individuals, since it provides a lot of value to the policyholder. With TROP plans, the policyholder gets to enjoy a guaranteed* return of the premiums that he paid. This reduces the financial burden significantly, which in turn gives the holder some much-needed peace of mind.
If you opt for a term plan with a return of premium option, make sure you pay your premiums regularly. That way, you will continue to enjoy the life cover, and in case you survive the policy term, all the premiums you paid will be returned, so you can count on that lump sum inflow of funds at the end of the tenure.
FAQs
1. Are There Any Riders Available With TROP?
Yes, riders like critical illness rider, waiver of premium rider, or accidental death benefit rider can be availed with a TROP plan at a nominal additional premium. However, the maturity amount paid on surviving the policy will not include the rider costs.
2. Is term insurance with a return of premium worth buying?
A TROP plan combines financial protection and savings under the same plan. It’s a favourable choice if your budget allows, and the peace of mind of getting premium back on survival matters.
3. Should unmarried individuals buy term insurance with return of premium?
Unmarried individuals should buy term insurance with return of premium if they’ve dependent parents or siblings. The sum assured on death during policy tenure will provide financial support. The refund of premiums on living till the end of the policy can give them a financial corpus for their needs.
4. Does term insurance with return of premium offer tax benefits?
Yes. Premiums paid under TROP qualify for deduction under Section 80C and the payout on maturity is exempt under Section 10(10D), subject to stipulations of the Income Tax Act , 1961 .
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
